directional-change-scaling-laws.title: Directional-change event framework yields 12 stable scaling laws in FX (Glattfelder-Dupuis-Olsen)
directional-change-scaling-laws.claim: Defining market structure by directional-change events (price reversals of threshold delta) instead of clock time yields 12 empirical scaling laws that hold across ~3 orders of magnitude of threshold size and across 13 currency pairs — e.g. average overshoot after a directional change scales lawfully with the threshold, giving a formal, parameter-stable definition of trend continuation vs reversal.

source.label: Glattfelder, Dupuis & Olsen (2008/2011), 'Patterns in high-frequency FX data: Discovery of 12 empirical scaling laws', Quantitative Finance 11(4), 599-614
source.quote: We have discovered 12 independent new empirical scaling laws in foreign exchange data-series that hold for close to three orders of magnitude and across 13 currency exchange rates. Our statistical analysis crucially depends on an event-based approach that measures the relationship between different types of events.

directional-change-scaling-laws.rule_formal: Directional change (DC) of threshold delta: price reverses by delta from the running extreme since the last DC; each DC is followed by an overshoot (OS) segment until the next DC. Scaling laws relate e.g. average OS size and durations to delta as power laws (exact coefficients in full text).

numbers.effect: 12 independent scaling laws; validity across ~3 orders of magnitude of threshold
numbers.sample: 13 currency exchange rates, high-frequency FX data
numbers.caveats: Abstract does not give the individual law coefficients (e.g. the overshoot~threshold relation) — full text needed for exact constants; gold not in the 13 rates, transferability must be re-estimated

directional-change-scaling-laws.mechanism: Not flow-based: statistical regularity of the price path itself across scales — implies the ratio of continuation (overshoot) to reversal (directional change) is stable and estimable, so deviation from the lawful overshoot is measurable in realtime
directional-change-scaling-laws.relevance: Q4/Q1: gives a formal, clock-free definition of trend vs range that 'declares itself' during the day — a breakout that has already produced a DC+overshoot beyond the lawful average is statistically extended, while one that stalls before the average overshoot is failing; candidate replacement for the falsified day-level trend-state (EMA-slope percentile) with an event-level definition

test_design.lookahead_risk: DC/OS state is computable tick-by-tick (running extremes only, no future data) — fully realtime; threshold delta must be fixed ex ante

directional-change-scaling-laws.overlap_risk: Overshoot magnitudes scale with volatility, so DC statistics computed per-day will correlate with dead rv variable; the event-level (within-trade) usage — has this breakout's overshoot exceeded its lawful expectation — is not captured by any of the five dead day-level variables

verification.biggest_finding: QUOTE ตรง verbatim ทุกคำบน arXiv abstract (Glattfelder, Dupuis, Olsen — 'Patterns in high-frequency FX data') · เลข 12 laws / ~3 orders of magnitude / 13 currency rates อยู่ในประโยคแรกของ abstract ครบ
verification.note: ส่วน '12 scaling laws ยืน ~3 orders, 13 คู่เงิน' ยืนยันได้ 100% จากหน้า abstract · แต่ส่วน 'นิยาม formal ของ trend continuation vs reversal' ไม่อยู่บนหน้า abstract — abstract พูดแค่ 'event-based approach' ลอยๆ นิยาม directional-change/overshoot อยู่ในตัว paper เต็มซึ่งหน้านี้ไม่แสดง · โดยรวมถือว่า claim ไม่บิด แต่ครึ่งหลังเป็นการสรุปจาก full text ไม่ใช่จากหน้าที่ fetch ได้

gap-day-momentum-us-indices.title: Prices continue in the gap direction on gap days; gaps do not systematically fill (Plastun et al.)
gap-day-momentum-us-indices.claim: On days with an opening price gap, prices tend to continue moving in the direction of the gap during that day (intraday momentum after gaps); the momentum effect is temporary (same-day, not multi-day), there is no seasonality in gap occurrence, and the popular belief that gaps get filled is not supported.

source.label: Plastun, Sibande, Gupta & Wohar (2020), 'Price gap anomaly in the US stock market: The whole story', North American Journal of Economics and Finance 52
source.quote: during a gap day prices tend to change in the direction of the gap

gap-day-momentum-us-indices.rule_formal: Gap day: Open_t != Close_{t-1} beyond a threshold; test H0: distribution of (Close_t - Open_t) sign/magnitude independent of sign(Open_t - Close_{t-1}); finding: E[Close_t - Open_t | gap up] > 0 and symmetric for gap down (continuation, not fill).

numbers.sample: 1928-2018, DJI, S&P 500 and NASDAQ (daily data)
numbers.caveats: Daily-frequency study on equity indices; abstract fetch gives direction of findings but no return magnitudes; does not state control for weekend/holiday gaps — the exact confound that killed the Trellis gap variable is not addressed in the abstract

gap-day-momentum-us-indices.mechanism: Gaps embody overnight information whose incorporation is incomplete at the open (orders placed before open only partially absorb the news); remaining underreaction resolves as same-direction intraday drift — momentum is temporary because it is a within-day incorporation process
gap-day-momentum-us-indices.relevance: Q3: supports SIGNED gap direction as an intraday directional conditioner (breakouts aligned with gap direction should follow through more) — distinct from the falsified unsigned |gap|/prev_range magnitude filter; also directly falsifies the gap-fill (fade) alternative, so the fade direction need not be tested

test_design.lookahead_risk: Gap sign known at day open (01:00 EET or first tick), hours before the 08:00-20:00 entry window — no lookahead

gap-day-momentum-us-indices.overlap_risk: High measurement overlap with dead variable |overnight gap|/prev_range (p=0.035 raw, dies controlling days-since-prev/weekend) — BUT dead variable was unsigned day filter, this is signed direction-alignment; any test must inherit the weekend/days-since-prev control that killed the unsigned version

gold-session-informed-uninformed.title: Gold intraday sessions differ: Tokyo uninformed vs New York informed trading (Iwatsubo-Watkins-Xu)
gold-session-informed-uninformed.claim: In gold futures, the Tokyo day session is dominated by uninformed trading while the New York day session is dominated by informed trading; microstructure character (efficiency, volatility, volume, liquidity) is session-specific across Tokyo/London/New York hours.

source.label: Iwatsubo, Kentaro; Watkins, Clinton; Xu, Tao (2017), 'Intraday Seasonality in Efficiency, Liquidity, Volatility and Volume: Platinum and gold futures in Tokyo and New York', Kobe University working paper 1722 (publ. Journal of Commodity Markets 2018)
source.quote: Separating global trading hours into Tokyo, London and New York day sessions, we also find that uninformed trading is more prevalent during the Tokyo day session while informed trading dominates the New York day session for both metals in both locations.

numbers.market: Gold and platinum futures traded in Tokyo (TOCOM) and New York (COMEX)
numbers.caveats: Abstract gives qualitative session ranking only; sample period and quantitative volatility/efficiency shapes are in full text (ScienceDirect 403; RIETI PDF not text-extractable in this mission)

gold-session-informed-uninformed.mechanism: Informed and uninformed traders self-select trading hours by venue characteristics: price-relevant information (US macro data, COMEX depth) arrives and is impounded during New York hours, while Asian-session flow is predominantly liquidity/hedging (uninformed), leaving Asian-hours price moves less informative about direction
gold-session-informed-uninformed.relevance: Q5 directly: supports the structural premise of Dual Asian BO — the Asian session (01:00–08:00 EET, overlapping Tokyo) is uninformed-flow range formation, and the breakout window (London/NY) is when informed flow sets direction; also cautions that late-NY moves may again be inefficient/noisy, relevant to holding until 23:00 EOD

test_design.lookahead_risk: Session labels are clock-based, fully known ex ante — no lookahead; testable as session-conditional follow-through rates of breakouts

gold-session-informed-uninformed.overlap_risk: Low overlap with dead variables (rv/slope/range_exp/gap/aw are all day-level magnitudes); this is a session-structure claim, closest to existing entry-window design itself rather than any tested filter

intraday-momentum-first-half-hour.title: Market intraday momentum: first half-hour return predicts last half-hour return (Gao-Han-Li-Zhou)
intraday-momentum-first-half-hour.claim: The return in the first half-hour of the session (measured from the previous day's close, i.e. including the overnight gap) positively predicts the return in the last half-hour of the same session; predictability is stronger on volatile, high-volume, recession, and macro-news days.

source.label: Gao, Han, Li & Zhou (2018), 'Market intraday momentum', Journal of Financial Economics 129(2), 394-414
source.quote: Based on high frequency S & P 500 exchange-traded fund (ETF) data from 1993–2013, we show an intraday momentum pattern: the first half-hour return on the market as measured from the previous day's market close predicts the last half-hour return.

intraday-momentum-first-half-hour.rule_formal: r_first = (P_10:00 - P_close_prev)/P_close_prev; r_last = (P_16:00 - P_15:30)/P_15:30; regression r_last = a + b*r_first, b > 0. Note r_first includes overnight gap by construction.

numbers.sample: S&P 500 ETF (SPY) high-frequency data, 1993–2013; pattern also present in ten other actively traded ETFs
numbers.caveats: Predictive R-squared and strategy returns are in the full paper (SSRN/ScienceDirect both 403 in this mission); abstract confirms direction and conditioning states only: stronger on volatile, high-volume, recession, and major macro news days

intraday-momentum-first-half-hour.mechanism: Two proposed channels: infrequent portfolio rebalancing (investors who rebalance late in the day trade in the direction of the early move) and late-informed trading on morning news near the close — both force same-direction flow at end of day
intraday-momentum-first-half-hour.relevance: Q1/Q4: gives a realtime early-session signal (first half-hour direction, gap included) that predicts late-session continuation — a candidate 'follow-through vs false break' discriminator: breakout aligned with strong first-half-hour return should follow through into EOD; Q5: pattern amplifies exactly on high-vol/high-volume days

test_design.lookahead_risk: First half-hour return known at 30 min after session open, well before Trellis entry window closes at 20:00 — no lookahead; but must fix which 'session open' concept maps to 24h gold (e.g. 08:00 EET or COMEX open)

intraday-momentum-first-half-hour.overlap_risk: r_first includes the overnight gap, so it partially correlates with dead variable |overnight gap|/prev_range; distinction: this is signed early return with continuation direction, not unsigned gap magnitude as a day filter

intraday-tsm-commodity-futures.title: Intraday time-series momentum extends to commodity futures; stronger on high early volume/volatility (Jin-Kearney-Li-Yang)
intraday-tsm-commodity-futures.claim: In commodity futures (copper, steel, soybean, soybean meal), the first half-hour return positively predicts the last half-hour return; in metals the effect strengthens when the early session has elevated volume or volatility, and a strategy trading the last half-hour in the first half-hour's direction beats buy-and-hold.

source.label: Jin, Kearney, Li & Yang (2019), 'Intraday time-series momentum: Evidence from China', Journal of Futures Markets 40(4), 632-650
source.quote: This study conducts an investigation of intraday time-series momentum across four Chinese commodity futures contracts: copper, steel, soybean, and soybean meal. Our results indicate that the first half-hour return positively predicts the last half-hour return across all four futures.

intraday-tsm-commodity-futures.rule_formal: r_last = a + b*r_first + e per contract, b > 0 for all four; interaction: b larger conditional on high first-half-hour volume or volatility (metals). Trade rule: position in last half-hour = sign(r_first).

numbers.sample: Four Chinese commodity futures (copper, steel, soybean, soybean meal); sample period not stated on landing page
numbers.caveats: Coefficients/R-squared in full text only; Chinese sessions have lunch breaks and (pre-2013 metals) no night session — session mapping to 23h gold differs materially

intraday-tsm-commodity-futures.mechanism: Day traders provide liquidity against the early informed move and must close positions before the session end to avoid overnight risk, forcing late-day flow in the direction of the early move — the same inventory-unwind channel proposed for equity ETFs, now shown in metals
intraday-tsm-commodity-futures.relevance: Q5/Q1: confirms intraday momentum is not an equity-only artifact — it survives in metal futures, the closest asset class to gold found with a fetchable academic test; the volume/volatility interaction repeats the Q2 'works when participation is abnormal' condition; supports testing early-window direction as EOD-continuation conditioner on XAUUSD

test_design.lookahead_risk: First-window return and its volume/volatility known in realtime; for gold must choose the analog windows ex ante (e.g. first hour after London or NY open vs final hour before 23:00 EOD close)

intraday-tsm-commodity-futures.overlap_risk: Volume/volatility interaction overlaps with dead rv variable at day level, but the conditioning here is same-day early-session (not prev-day) and the core signal (signed early return) was never among the five falsified variables

orb-crude-oil-profitability.title: ORB on crude oil futures beats fair-game benchmark (Holmberg et al.)
orb-crude-oil-profitability.claim: An Opening Range Breakout strategy that trades only when price moves beyond a predetermined threshold (calibrated from the assumed return distribution) yields returns significantly above zero and a success rate above the fair-game benchmark on US crude oil futures.

source.label: Holmberg, Lönnbark & Lundström (2012), Umeå Economic Studies 845 / Finance Research Letters 2013, 'Assessing the profitability of intraday opening range breakout strategies'
source.quote: An investor that trades on the ORB strategy seeks to identify large intraday price movements and trades only when the price moves beyond some predetermined threshold. We present an ORB strategy based on normally distributed returns to identify such days and find that our ORB trading strategy result in significantly higher returns than zero as well as an increased success rate in relation to a fair game.

orb-crude-oil-profitability.rule_formal: Enter long (short) when intraday price exceeds Open + k*sigma (Open - k*sigma), where k is a predetermined threshold derived from the assumed (normal) return distribution; hold to close. Exact k values not given in abstract.

numbers.market: US crude oil futures (per paper keywords; not stated in abstract text)
numbers.caveats: Abstract provides no effect sizes, success-rate figures, or sample period; full text paywalled at Finance Research Letters

orb-crude-oil-profitability.mechanism: Days with large early moves beyond a distribution-based threshold identify conditional fat-tail days; once threshold is crossed, the day's return distribution is right-shifted in the breakout direction (joint distribution of Low/High/Open/Close, not independence of increments)
orb-crude-oil-profitability.relevance: Q2 directly: academic evidence that ORB works when the trigger threshold is scaled to the return distribution (volatility-normalized), not a fixed range — suggests testing Asian-range breakout with distribution-calibrated trigger distance rather than raw range edge

test_design.lookahead_risk: Threshold computable from historical return distribution before session open; breakout observed realtime — no lookahead

orb-crude-oil-profitability.overlap_risk: Threshold scaling by recent volatility correlates with dead variable rv (prev-day realized-vol percentile); however the claim is about entry trigger geometry, not day filtering, so overlap is partial

orb-stocks-in-play-relative-volume.title: ORB works when restricted to instruments with abnormal relative volume (Zarattini-Barbon-Aziz)
orb-stocks-in-play-relative-volume.claim: Opening range breakout profitability concentrates in 'stocks in play' — instruments with unusually high trading activity that day due to fundamental news; relative volume measured in the opening range positively correlates with subsequent breakout P&L, and restricting ORB trades to the highest relative-volume names is what makes the strategy profitable net of costs.

source.label: Zarattini, Barbon & Aziz (2024), 'A Profitable Day Trading Strategy For The U.S. Equity Market', SSRN 4729284 (authors' summary page)
source.quote: These are stocks experiencing unusually high trading activity due to company-specific news, earnings announcements, or other fundamental developments.

orb-stocks-in-play-relative-volume.rule_formal: RelVol = volume in first 5 minutes today / average first-5-minute volume over prior days; rank universe by RelVol at open; trade 5-minute ORB (long above OR high / short below OR low) only on top-ranked names; stop and EOD exit per paper.

numbers.effect: Top-20 stocks-in-play portfolio: >1,600% total net return, Sharpe 2.81, annualized alpha 36% (vs S&P 500 ~198% same period); beta ~0
numbers.sample: 2016-2023, more than 7,000 US stocks
numbers.caveats: Equities cross-section with name-selection dimension gold does not have; numbers from authors' own summary page of the SSRN paper (SSRN itself 403); working paper, not peer-reviewed journal

orb-stocks-in-play-relative-volume.mechanism: A fundamental catalyst forces repricing: news-driven participants must build/liquidate positions within the day, generating persistent one-directional flow after the opening range resolves; without a catalyst (normal volume) the breakout has no forced flow behind it and mean-reverts
orb-stocks-in-play-relative-volume.relevance: Q2 directly: the condition under which ORB works is abnormal participation, measured as relative volume during range formation; for XAUUSD the analog is tick-count/volume of the Asian range or of the breakout bar relative to trailing same-hour average — a realtime Q1 discriminator candidate that is NOT among the five falsified day-level price variables

test_design.lookahead_risk: Relative volume computable at range end (08:00) or at breakout bar from M1 tick-counts vs trailing same-hour baseline — fully known at entry

orb-stocks-in-play-relative-volume.overlap_risk: Volume proxies correlate with realized volatility, so partial overlap with dead rv (prev-day vol percentile); key difference: rv was previous-day price volatility, this is same-day participation measured before/at entry — untested dimension (tick-count never used in the five falsified variables)

verification.biggest_finding: เลข >1,600% net / Sharpe 2.81 / alpha 36%/ปี (Top-20 stocks in play, 2016-2023) อยู่บนหน้า Concretum จริง — แต่ 'beta ~0' ไม่ปรากฏบนหน้านี้เลย (คำว่า beta ไม่มีในหน้า) น่าจะมาจาก SSRN paper ต้นทาง ไม่ใช่หน้าที่อ้าง
verification.note: QUOTE ('These are stocks experiencing unusually high trading activity...') ตรง verbatim · หน้านี้คือ paper ของ Zarattini-Barbon-Aziz ชื่อ 'A Profitable Day Trading Strategy For The U.S. Equity Market' (ไม่ใช่ 'Beat the Market' ซึ่งเป็น paper QQQ คนละฉบับ) · จุดอ่อนที่ harvester ไม่บอก: หน้านี้ไม่แสดง baseline แบบไม่กรอง (ทั้ง 7,000 ตัว) เลย — ประโยคจริงคือ 'restricting trades...significantly improves performance even after transaction costs' = 'improve' ไม่ใช่หลักฐานตรงว่า filter 'คือสิ่งที่ทำให้ net กำไร' · และหน้าไม่ผูก 1,600% กับ timeframe 5-min อย่างชัดเจน (ศึกษา 5/15/30/60-min)

overnight-drift-session-open.title: Overnight drift: equity premium concentrates at European open, driven by prior-close order imbalance (Boyarchenko-Larsen-Whelan)
overnight-drift-session-open.claim: In 24h-traded equity index futures, nearly the entire unconditional risk premium accrues in the 2:00-3:00 a.m. EST hour (around the European open); the drift is predictable from the previous US close's order imbalance, with an asymmetry — selloffs at the prior close generate robust positive overnight reversals, rallies generate much weaker ones.

source.label: Boyarchenko, Larsen & Whelan (2020), 'The Overnight Drift', CEPR DP14462 / NY Fed Staff Report 917
source.quote: almost 100 percent of the U.S. equity premium is earned during a one-hour window between 2:00 a.m. and 3:00 a.m. (EST)

overnight-drift-session-open.rule_formal: Partition the 24h day into hourly returns of S&P 500 futures; mean return of the 02:00-03:00 EST hour ≈ full-day unconditional mean; conditional version: overnight drift is negatively related to end-of-day order imbalance (stronger positive drift after sell-imbalance closes).

numbers.effect: ~100% of the US equity premium earned in the single 02:00-03:00 EST hour
numbers.sample: US equity index futures with round-the-clock electronic trading since the mid-1990s
numbers.caveats: Documented on equities, not gold; a 2026 NY Fed follow-up notes the drift has weakened ('The Disappearing Overnight Drift', not fetched); daylight-saving shifts move the effect with the session clock, not the wall clock

overnight-drift-session-open.mechanism: Dealer/market-maker inventory absorbed at the prior US close is offloaded when liquidity next becomes available — at Asian and especially European opens; the compensation for holding that inventory is earned in the hour when the offsetting flow arrives (Grossman-Miller inventory risk framework)
overnight-drift-session-open.relevance: Q3: this is the cleanest academic template for 'overnight flow → predictable session-open move' with an explicit conditioning variable (prior-day closing imbalance/direction) instead of raw gap size — suggests testing prior-NY-close direction (last-hour return) as a directional conditioner for Asian-range breakout side; Q5: session-open hours are structurally special in 24h markets, supporting clock-anchored (EET) session logic

test_design.lookahead_risk: Prior-day last-hour return and overnight hourly returns fully known before 08:00 EET entry window — no lookahead; DST handling must follow session clock (known Trellis EU-DST discipline)

overnight-drift-session-open.overlap_risk: Correlates with dead variable |overnight gap|/prev_range only weakly: that variable was unsigned magnitude with weekend confound; this claim is signed, hour-localized, and conditioned on prior-close imbalance direction, which was never tested

verification.biggest_finding: COUNTER-EVIDENCE ใหญ่ที่ harvester ไม่บอก: ผู้เขียนชุดเดียวกัน (Boyarchenko-Larsen-Whelan) ออก NY Fed Liberty Street blog ก.ค. 2026 'The Disappearing Overnight Drift' — หน้าต่าง 02:00-03:00 ที่เคยให้ ~3.7%/ปี 'averaged close to zero since 2021' เพราะ closing order imbalance บีบตัวจาก SD 6.5% เหลือ 2.9% — edge นี้ตายแล้วในข้อมูล 2021-2025
verification.note: QUOTE ตรง verbatim บนหน้า CBS Research Portal · asymmetry ยืนยัน: selloff ให้ 'robust positive overnight reversals' ส่วน rally ให้ reversal เบากว่ามาก · version สุดท้ายตีพิมพ์ Review of Financial Studies 36(9) 2023 pp.3502-3547 (หน้า CBS ที่ fetch เป็น CEPR DP 2020) เลขหลัก 'almost 100 percent...2:00-3:00 a.m.' ตรงกันทุก version ที่เช็ค · สำหรับ Trellis: อย่าใช้ effect นี้เป็น edge ปัจจุบัน — หลักฐานล่าสุดชี้ว่าหายไปตั้งแต่ 2021

stop-loss-cascade-breakout.title: Stop-loss order clusters make trends unusually rapid past trigger levels (Osler)
stop-loss-cascade-breakout.claim: Price trends accelerate abnormally when the rate reaches levels where stop-loss orders cluster (e.g. just beyond round numbers / technical levels); the response to stop-loss triggering is larger and lasts longer than the response to take-profit orders, producing self-reinforcing cascades in the breakout direction.

source.label: Osler, C.L. (2005), 'Stop-loss orders and price cascades in currency markets', Journal of International Money and Finance 24(2), 219-241
source.quote: Exchange rate trends are unusually rapid when rates reach exchange rate levels at which stop-loss order have been documented to cluster.

stop-loss-cascade-breakout.rule_formal: Conditional on price crossing a level L where stop orders cluster (documented: round numbers; ends of recent ranges), E[|r_{t,t+h}| in crossing direction] is elevated vs unconditional; response(stop-loss crossing) > response(take-profit level touch) in size and duration.

numbers.market: Major currency pairs (high-frequency exchange rates); order data from a large FX dealer
numbers.caveats: Abstract gives direction and ordering of effects, not magnitudes; effects documented at intraday horizons (hours), not days

stop-loss-cascade-breakout.mechanism: Stop-loss orders are positive-feedback trades: crossing the level forces dealers to buy into a rising market / sell into a falling one; clustered stops at obvious levels (range extremes, round numbers) create a queue of forced same-direction flow — this is who is 'forced to buy' after a genuine range break
stop-loss-cascade-breakout.relevance: Q1: gives the causal mechanism separating follow-through from false break — follow-through requires a populated stop cluster beyond the range edge; implies measurable proxies: proximity of Asian range edge to round numbers (e.g. $5/$10 gold grid), and speed of traverse immediately after the break (cascade signature = unusually rapid move in first minutes)

test_design.lookahead_risk: Round-number distance known at entry; post-break traverse speed measurable in first N minutes after signal — usable realtime only if entry is delayed or managed after trigger

stop-loss-cascade-breakout.overlap_risk: Post-break speed correlates with day volatility (dead rv variable) at day level, but is measured at event level (minutes around the break), which none of the five dead day-level variables capture; round-number proximity is orthogonal to all five

gold-overnight-vs-day-returns.title: Gold: overnight returns significantly positive, day-session returns significantly negative (30-year sample)
gold-overnight-vs-day-returns.claim: Across COMEX front gold futures, London Fix spot, gold miners and gold ETFs, overnight (non-US-day) returns are significantly positive while day-session returns are significantly negative, over roughly 30 years and in both up and down gold markets — i.e., the Asia/overnight period systematically gains and the London/NY day session systematically bleeds.

source.label: 'Overnight versus day returns in gold and gold related assets', Journal of Economics and Finance (2017), Springer
source.quote: Overnight returns are significantly positive while day returns are significantly negative

gold-overnight-vs-day-returns.rule_formal: r_overnight = close_prev_day_session -> open_next_day_session; r_day = open -> close of day session. E[r_overnight] > 0, E[r_day] < 0, both significant; asymmetry present in bull and bear subperiods and 'economically important even with transaction costs' (per abstract summary).

numbers.effect: Sign asymmetry only from abstract (positive overnight vs negative day); magnitudes are behind paywall
numbers.sample: ~30-year period; COMEX gold front futures, London Fix spot, gold mining stocks, gold closed-end funds and ETFs
numbers.caveats: Abstract-level access — effect sizes not extractable; 'day' is defined by US/COMEX session, which maps only partially onto our 08:00-20:00 server breakout window; sample ends ~2016, overlap with losing years 2017-2019 untested in this paper

gold-overnight-vs-day-returns.mechanism: Authors frame it as gold being '(too) high at the opening' of day markets. Candidate forced-flow reading (flagged as interpretation, not from the paper): commercial supply (producer hedging, fix-related selling) concentrates in London/NY business hours while Asian physical demand accumulates overnight — consistent with the PM-fix downward drift claim (micro-lbma-pmfix-downward-drift) but the paper itself does not identify who is forced.
gold-overnight-vs-day-returns.relevance: Q4/Q2: implies a structural long-bias overnight/Asia and short-bias during the day session — directly testable against our long vs short breakout PnL split by session and by era (did the day-session bleed weaken after 2015 fix reform / 2017-2019?)

test_design.lookahead_risk: Session boundaries are clock-based, known ex ante — a 'day-session short bias' prior needs no lookahead; but as an unconditional drift it is a weak per-trade signal

gold-overnight-vs-day-returns.overlap_risk: Day/overnight split partially overlaps gap (overnight return IS a gap-family variable — gap was tested dead at day level, so any value must come from session-conditional direction, not gap magnitude); orthogonal to rv/slope/range_exp/aw

verification.biggest_finding: ตัวเลข '~30 ปี' ใน claim ไม่มีอยู่ใน abstract ที่เข้าถึงได้เลย (abstract ไม่ระบุช่วงเวลา) — Springer เข้าไม่ได้ (timeout + auth-wall redirect) ต้อง verify ผ่าน mirror IDEAS/RePEc ซึ่งยืนยันประโยค sign-asymmetry verbatim แต่ยืนยันช่วงปีไม่ได้
verification.note: ประโยค 'Overnight returns are significantly positive while day returns are significantly negative' เป็นประโยคเปิด abstract จริง verbatim (Blose, Gondhalekar & Kort, Journal of Economics and Finance 2018) ครอบ COMEX gold futures, London Fix spot, gold mining stocks, CEFs, ETFs → sign asymmetry รองรับ claim ('ทุก asset' = เฉพาะ 5 กลุ่ม gold-related ใน paper ไม่ใช่ทุก asset ทั่วไป) · numbers_match ให้ false เพราะ '~30 ปี' ตรวจไม่ได้จากแหล่งที่ fetch ได้ · caveat ที่ harvester ไม่บอก: (1) overnight drift ที่วัดจาก OHLC เสี่ยง artifact จาก stale/auction open price (QuantPedia แสดงกรณี GDX) (2) งานหลัง 2017 ยังพบ night effect ในทอง (เช่น Chinese gold/silver futures 2025) แต่ชี้กลไก intraday reversal/anchoring มากกว่า risk premium — persistence ของ magnitude หลัง 2017 ยังไม่ถูกยืนยันโดย paper นี้

lbma-pmfix-downward-drift.title: Large moves in the London PM gold fix window were overwhelmingly DOWN, 2004-2013 (LBMA Alchemist debate of Abrantes-Metz/Metz)
lbma-pmfix-downward-drift.claim: During 2004-2013, large price moves inside the London PM fixing window (15:00 London = 17:00 EET server) were downward at least two-thirds of the time in six different years, peaking at 92% downward in 2010 — a systematic directional pressure at a fixed clock time inside the strategy's breakout window.

source.label: LBMA Alchemist issue 73, 'Has There Been a Decade of London PM Gold Fixing Manipulation?' (discussing Abrantes-Metz & Metz study; rebuttal by Peter Fertig)
source.quote: large price moves during the fix were downwards at least two-thirds of the time in six different years between 2004 and 2013 ... In 2010 ... large moves during the fix were negative 92 percent of the time.

lbma-pmfix-downward-drift.rule_formal: Window W = PM fixing interval starting 15:00 London (17:00 server EET, constant offset since UK and EET share EU DST dates). Condition on |return in W| > threshold (large move): P(down) >= 2/3 in 6 of the years 2004-2013; 0.92 in 2010. Testable on M1: sign distribution of large 17:00-17:15 server moves by year.

numbers.effect: Large fix-window moves downward >= 2/3 of the time in six different years; 92% downward in 2010
numbers.sample: Intraday data 2001-2013; anomalous pattern appears 'from 2004 until the end of the data sample'
numbers.market: London gold PM fixing vs spot/futures intraday
numbers.caveats: Fertig rebuttal in same article: patterns 'could be well explained and are not a valid proof of manipulations' (US data releases, commercial composition of fix participants). Regime-bound: the 5-bank fixing was replaced by the electronic LBMA Gold Price auction in March 2015 — effect may be dead after 2015. Both explanations (collusion vs producer-selling composition) still imply the same testable directional drift pre-2015.

lbma-pmfix-downward-drift.mechanism: Clients transacting AT the fix (miners/producers hedging, refiners) are structurally net sellers at the PM benchmark; the fixing banks who absorb that imbalance hedge/pre-hedge during (or per the manipulation hypothesis, ahead of) the window — either way the forced side is fix-bank hedging of a seller-heavy client imbalance at a known clock time.
lbma-pmfix-downward-drift.relevance: Q2 directly (PM fix systematic direction with published numbers). Losing years 2012/2014 fall inside the documented 2004-2013/15 anomaly regime; 2017-2019 fall after the 2015 reform — testable whether short-side breakouts near 17:00 server behaved differently across the two regimes (also feeds Q4 era question).

test_design.lookahead_risk: Fix time is clock-known ex ante; a '17:00-server proximity/direction' feature is computable at entry with zero lookahead; year-regime split (pre/post-2015) known ex ante

lbma-pmfix-downward-drift.overlap_risk: A fix-window drift feature is time-of-day anchored — orthogonal to day-level rv/slope/range_exp/gap/aw; mild interaction with rv because large-move conditioning selects volatile afternoons

verification.biggest_finding: ท่อนแรกของ quote ถูก splice โดยไม่ใส่ ellipsis: ข้อความจริงคือ 'On days when the authors identified large price moves during the fix, they were downwards at least two-thirds of the time...' — harvester ตัด 'On days when the authors identified' และ ', they' ทิ้งแล้วต่อประโยคใหม่ · และตัวเลขทั้งหมดเป็น second-hand: บทความ (Fertig) แค่ยก Bloomberg ที่รายงาน draft study ของ Abrantes-Metz มาเพื่อ 'หักล้าง'
verification.note: ตัวเลข ≥2/3 ใน 6 ปี (2004–2013) และ 92% ปี 2010 มีอยู่ในหน้าจริง (ประโยค 92% verbatim เป๊ะ) → numbers_match ผ่าน แต่ quote_found ให้ fail เพราะท่อน two-thirds ถูกเรียบเรียงใหม่โดยไม่ mark การตัด · context สำคัญ: บทความทั้งชิ้นเป็น rebuttal — Fertig ไม่เถียงตัวเลขแต่เถียงการตีความ โดยอธิบาย downward skew ด้วย flow composition (central banks/producers ขายที่ PM fix ส่วนผู้ซื้อไม่พึ่ง fix) + US econ data 10:00 NY ชนหน้าต่าง fix · regime เจ้าของ pattern จบแล้ว: PM fix แบบโทรศัพท์ถูกแทนด้วย LBMA Gold Price auction มี.ค. 2015 (บทความไม่กล่าวถึง) — drift 2004–2013 ห้าม extrapolate หลัง 2015 และถ้าคำอธิบายคือ flow composition pattern ก็ตายได้เมื่อ composition เปลี่ยน

osler-reversal-vs-continuation-duration.title: Bounce at a level fades within ~30 minutes; post-crossing trend persists 2+ hours (Osler full text)
osler-reversal-vs-continuation-duration.claim: The reversal tendency after touching a salient level is statistically detectable for less than 30 minutes, whereas the extra trend speed after CROSSING the level stays statistically significant for at least two hours — i.e., false-break reversals are fast/short-lived and real breaks show persistent excess drift measurable on minute bars.

source.label: Osler, C.L., 'Stop-Loss Orders and Price Cascades in Currency Markets', FRBNY Staff Report 150 (full text PDF, Georgetown mirror)
source.quote: the tendency to reverse course at round numbers remains statistically significant less than thirty minutes (Table VIII). By contrast, the average exchange-rate movement upon crossing a round number remains statistically significant for at least two hours.

osler-reversal-vs-continuation-duration.rule_formal: Event = price comes within 0.01% of level L. Split: (a) touch-no-cross -> excess reversal probability vs 10,000 arbitrary-level bootstrap, significant only at horizons < 30 min; (b) cross -> excess movement in crossing direction (Table VI.A: excess movement at 15 min = 1.12 (DEM), 1.30 (JPY), 0.77 (GBP), all p=0.000) significant up to ~2 h; sign flips negative at 1-2 day horizon.

numbers.effect: Post-crossing excess movement at 15 min: DEM 1.12, JPY 1.30, GBP 0.77 (bootstrap p=0.000 all three); still significant at 30-120 min; reversal effect at touched-but-not-crossed levels dies out <30 min; at 1-2 days excess movement turns negative (e.g. DEM -5.23 at 1 day)
numbers.sample: Minute-by-minute quotes, DEM/JPY/GBP vs USD, 9am-4pm New York, 2 Jan 1996 - 30 Apr 1998; 58 non-overlapping 10-trading-day intervals vs 10,000 bootstrap sets of arbitrary levels
numbers.caveats: Excess-movement units are relative to arbitrary-level baseline (ratio-like bootstrap statistic, not pips); FX 1996-98, not gold; NY-hours only; the 1-2 day sign flip warns that cascade drift is an intraday phenomenon, consistent with EOD-close design

osler-reversal-vs-continuation-duration.mechanism: After a crossing, stop-loss owners beyond the level are forced to trade with the move and are 'sometimes triggered in waves' (cascades) — sequential forced flow keeps the trend statistically alive for hours. A touch without crossing only activates voluntary take-profit flow, which exhausts quickly, so the bounce signature is short.
osler-reversal-vs-continuation-duration.relevance: Q1: gives a time-domain signature for real-vs-false break — measure excess drift persistence in the 15-120 min window after Asian-range breakout; a break that stalls within 30 min behaves like the touch/reversal population. Also supports holding winners through the session rather than scalping the first minutes.

test_design.lookahead_risk: Persistence measured AFTER entry cannot gate the entry itself; usable for exit/management rules (e.g. time-stop at 30 min if no follow-through) or for delayed confirmation entries — must be encoded as management, not entry filter, to avoid lookahead

osler-reversal-vs-continuation-duration.overlap_risk: Same source family as academic-stop-loss-cascade-breakout (adds duration/horizon numbers absent there); a 30-min follow-through gauge will correlate with intraday rv on the same day but is event-anchored, not day-level; unrelated to slope/gap/aw

verification.biggest_finding: หน่วยที่ harvester ละไว้ = basis points ไม่ใช่ pips/percent — ข้อความใน paper เทียบเอง: 'dollar-yen moves by 0.0130 percent more after crossing a round number than after reversing course' = ค่า 1.30 ในตาราง VI.A → excess ทั้งหมดอยู่ระดับ ~1 bp/15 นาที เล็กมากเชิงเศรษฐศาสตร์ และตัวเลข 1.12/1.30/0.77 เป็น 'ส่วนต่าง crossing ลบ reversing' (MV_CR − MV_RV) ไม่ใช่ raw drift หลังทะลุ
verification.note: PDF จริงคือ 'Stop-Loss Orders and Price Cascades in Currency Markets' (Osler) — quote พบ verbatim และตาราง VI.A ตรงทุกตัว (15min: DEM 1.12/JPY 1.30/GBP 0.77 sig 0.000 ทั้งสาม · 1 วัน DEM −5.23). แต่ต้องติดป้าย: (1) significance จางเร็ว — 1 ชม. GBP หลุดแล้ว (p=0.179), 2 ชม. เหลือแค่ JPY (p=0.017); ประโยค 'at least two hours' มาจากบริบท Table VIII (stop-loss vs take-profit persistence) ไม่ใช่ทุกคู่เงินใน VI.A (2) ที่ 1 วัน JPY ยังบวก +1.27 — 'กลับเป็นลบที่ 1-2 วัน' ไม่ครบทุกคู่ และค่าลบเหล่านั้นส่วนใหญ่ไม่ significant (p 0.074–0.552) (3) สนามวัด = Reuters minute quotes ม.ค. 1996–เม.ย. 1998 เฉพาะ NY hours 9:00–16:00 สามคู่ FX — generalize มา gold 24 ชม. ยุค 2012-2020 คือ extrapolation ล้วนๆ

osler-stop-cluster-asymmetry.title: Stop-loss orders cluster just BEYOND round levels; take-profit orders cluster AT them (Osler full text, order-book data)
osler-stop-cluster-asymmetry.claim: In dealer order books, stop-loss buy orders cluster just above round levels and stop-loss sell orders just below, while take-profit orders cluster exactly at the level. Therefore a touch of a salient level meets negative-feedback (reversal) flow first, and only a traverse PAST the level (into the [01,10]-style zone beyond) triggers the positive-feedback stop flow that fuels a real break.

source.label: Osler, C.L., 'Stop-Loss Orders and Price Cascades in Currency Markets', FRBNY Staff Report 150 (full text PDF, Georgetown mirror)
source.quote: stop-loss buy orders cluster just above round numbers (meaning specifically numbers ending in 00 or 50), and stop-loss sell orders tend to cluster at rates just below round numbers. For example, 14.3 percent of executed stop-loss buy orders have requested execution rates ending in the range [01,10], while only 6.9 percent of those orders have requested execution rates ending in the range [90,99].

osler-stop-cluster-asymmetry.rule_formal: Let L = salient level (round number; by extension a visible range extreme). Order mass: TP(L) > SL(L) at exactly L (TP 9.9% vs SL 3.8% at rates ending 00, value-weighted); SL mass peaks in zone (L, L+10pts] for buys / [L-10pts, L) for sells. Prediction: touch-without-traverse of L -> mean reversion; traverse beyond L by more than the TP-absorption zone -> positive-feedback continuation.

numbers.effect: 14.3% of executed stop-loss buys sit in [01,10] beyond the round level vs 6.9% in [90,99] before it; take-profit orders exactly at 00: 9.9% vs stop-loss 3.8% (value-weighted); ~10% of all orders at rates ending 00
numbers.sample: 9,655 stop-loss/take-profit orders, >$55bn face value, complete order book of a major FX dealing bank, 1 Aug 1999 - 11 Apr 2000; USD/JPY, GBP/USD, EUR/USD
numbers.market: Spot FX (major pairs), dealer order book
numbers.caveats: FX not gold; 1999-2000 vintage; levels studied are round numbers ending 00/50 — mapping to Asian high/low is an extrapolation (both are salient reference levels, but Asian range extremes were not directly tested)
numbers.note_verbatim_secondary: Very-large stops concentrate hardest near levels: 'Over 62 percent of the total face value of very-large stop-loss orders had requested execution rates ending in [90, 100] or [01,09]' vs 28% for very-large take-profits (p.26 of PDF)

osler-stop-cluster-asymmetry.mechanism: Traders holding positions with stops just beyond the level are forced to buy (sell) at market the moment the level is traversed — positive feedback. Take-profit/limit holders AT the level voluntarily trade against the move — negative feedback. Who is forced: stop-order owners beyond the level (and dealers executing them), in waves, only after traverse; at the level itself nobody is forced and limit supply dominates.
osler-stop-cluster-asymmetry.relevance: Q1 (core): gives an order-book-based reason why a marginal poke through the Asian high can reverse (TP/limit absorption zone AT the level) while a traverse deep enough to eat through the beyond-level stop cluster continues. Suggests a penetration-depth threshold variable, and a round-number-confluence variable (Asian high/low near $X.00/xx50 gold levels should carry bigger stop clusters).

test_design.lookahead_risk: Penetration depth beyond Asian high/low and distance of the level to nearest round number are both computable at/before entry from M1 OHLC — no lookahead if entry waits for a fixed penetration threshold

osler-stop-cluster-asymmetry.overlap_risk: Complementary to inbox claim academic-stop-loss-cascade-breakout (same Osler literature, abstract-level, numbers null) — this adds full-text cluster-location numbers; penetration-depth is event-level, weakly related to day-level range_exp; round-number distance orthogonal to rv/slope/range_exp/gap/aw

verification.biggest_finding: Dataset = order book ของ FX dealing bank รายใหญ่ 'แห่งเดียว' (paper ไม่ระบุชื่อ — คำถาม NatWest ยืนยันจากไฟล์นี้ไม่ได้) ช่วง 1 ส.ค. 1999–11 เม.ย. 2000 (8.5 เดือน) 9,655 orders มูลค่า $55B ใน dollar-yen/dollar-pound/euro-dollar — ไม่มีทองคำ ไม่มีปี 2012-2020 · และ '~10% ของ orders ลงท้าย 00' ที่ harvester เขียน จริงๆ เป็นเลขของ take-profit orders เท่านั้น (9.9% value-weighted; stop-loss แค่ 3.8%)
verification.note: Quote พบ verbatim ครบทั้งท่อน รวม 14.3% vs 6.9% ([01,10] vs [90,99]) และ TP-at-00 9.9% vs SL 3.8% ตรงตาม Table III. กลไกที่ claim สรุป (TP กองที่ level → reversal flow / SL กองเลย level → เร่ง break) ตรง thesis 'price cascades' ของ paper. Counter-nuance: significance ของ asymmetry รอบ 00 ไม่แข็งเท่ากันทุกฝั่ง — SL buy p=0.028 แต่ SL sell แค่ marginal p=0.063 (รอบ 50 แข็งกว่า: 0.002/0.005). การ generalize order-clustering ของ FX dealer ยุค 1999-2000 (ก่อน algo/retail-broker era) มาเป็นสมมติฐาน flow บนทองคำทศวรรษ 2010s ต้องถือเป็น analogy ไม่ใช่ evidence ตรง

prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay.title: NR7 + Opening Range Breakout: edge does not survive trading costs (42 US futures, 1980-2016)
prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay.claim: Crabel's NR7 setup traded via opening-range-breakout stretch entry, tested across 42 US futures 1980-2016, is not tradeable after transaction costs without additional filter rules; the pattern works better with NR_Length >= 6 and longer holding periods.

source.label: Oxford Capital Strategies — NR7 Pattern | Trading Strategy (Setup & Exit)
source.quote: The current daily range is narrower than the previous six days' daily ranges compared individually ... A buy stop is placed at [Open + Stretch] ... The NR7 pattern performs better when NR_Length >= 6 ... Once the cost of trading is applied, the pattern is not currently tradeable without some additional rules

prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay.rule_formal: NR7: Range(t) < min(Range(t-1..t-6)). Entry next day: buy stop = Open + Stretch, sell stop = Open - Stretch, Stretch = avg(10-day noise) * 2.0. Exits tested: (a) close on Nth day, (b) opposite stretch level, (c) stop = Entry -/+ ATR(20) * 6.
prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay.mechanism: Range contraction compresses positioning; the first directional move from the open forces breakout entries in one direction — but per this test the per-trade edge is smaller than realistic costs.
prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay.relevance: Q1 (breakout entry off a compression day = same family as Asian-range breakout; negative cost result is a prior against adding NR-style day filters expecting large edge), Q2 (NR7 as day-before trend-day antecedent)

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — NR7 known at prior day close; stretch computable at day open

prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay.overlap_risk: high overlap with rv percentile and asian width ratio (both are range-contraction measures already falsified on this system)

prac-crabel-nr-orb-winrates-secondary.title: Crabel (1990) NR4/NR7 -> ORB win rates 60-76% (secondary summary, primary tables not verified)
prac-crabel-nr-orb-winrates-secondary.claim: Per a secondary summary of Crabel's 1990 book, opening-range-breakout trades taken after NR4/NR7 compression days showed win rates of 60-76% in his pre-1990 futures backtests, with inside-day combinations (IDnr4, NR7ID) among the strongest variants.

source.label: TradingStrategiesDaily — The NR7ID: Toby Crabel's 'Double-Compression' Trading Setup
source.quote: NR patterns (NR4/NR7) improved ORB reliability, with win rates often 60-76% in tested setups.

prac-crabel-nr-orb-winrates-secondary.rule_formal: NR4: Range(t) < min(Range(t-1..t-3)). NR7: Range(t) < min(Range(t-1..t-6)). IDnr4/NR7ID: additionally High(t) < High(t-1) AND Low(t) > Low(t-1). Trade: next-day ORB stop entry beyond open +/- stretch.

numbers.effect: win rates 60-76% for ORB after NR patterns
numbers.sample: Crabel pre-1990 backtests on futures (S&P, bonds, currencies, commodities); per-setup n not given in this source
numbers.caveats: secondary paraphrase — primary per-table numbers from the book not independently verified online; pre-1990 markets; directly contradicted on net-of-cost basis by oxfordstrat 1980-2016 test (see prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay); win rate without avg win/loss says nothing about expectancy

prac-crabel-nr-orb-winrates-secondary.mechanism: Compression day = neither side committed; next-day directional move from open finds little resting opposition and forces re-positioning in one direction.
prac-crabel-nr-orb-winrates-secondary.relevance: Q2 (day-before compression as trend-day antecedent with a published-number lineage), Q1 (ORB stretch entry as breakout confirmation distance)

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — pattern known at prior close

prac-crabel-nr-orb-winrates-secondary.overlap_risk: same family as asian width ratio / rv percentile (compression measures, falsified) — main incremental content is the day-boundary anchor (daily bars) instead of Asian-session anchor

verification.biggest_finding: เลข 60-76% มีอยู่จริงบนหน้า แต่หน้าไม่อ้าง table/หน้าไหนของหนังสือ Crabel เลย (secondary ไร้ citation) และประโยค 'Modern adaptations confirm the edge' ขัดกับ study อิสระ oxfordstrat: NR7 บน 42 futures markets, 36 ปีตั้งแต่ 1980 ถึง ม.ค. 2016 สรุปว่า 'Once the cost of trading is applied, the pattern is not currently tradeable without some additional rules' (rating C)
verification.note: QUOTE ตรง verbatim และ claim เรา frame เป็น secondary summary ถูกต้องแล้ว แต่ต้องย้ำ: oxfordstrat กับ Crabel เป็นคนละ metric (net expectancy หลัง costs vs raw win%) จึงไม่หักล้างเลข win% ตรงๆ ทว่ามันหักล้างนัยว่า edge ยังใช้ได้หลัง costs · win 60-76% pre-1990 ไม่มีทาง audit ได้จากหน้านี้ — ให้น้ำหนักเป็น 'เลขเล่าต่อ' เท่านั้น ห้ามใช้เป็น expectancy input ของ Trellis

prac-mnq-ohlcv-falsification.title: Systematic falsification: OHLCV-only intraday signals (ORB, gaps, liquidity grabs) leave 0.07-1.50 pts/trade gross on MNQ — none pass all criteria
prac-mnq-ohlcv-falsification.claim: On MNQ 5-minute data 2021-2025 (947 trading days), a systematic test of opening range breakouts, gap strategies, volume signals, cross-session momentum, liquidity grabs, and volatility-conditioned classifiers found gross next-bar-open edge of only ~0.07-1.50 points per trade, and no signal satisfied all robustness criteria simultaneously.

source.label: arXiv 2605.04004 — Structural Limits of OHLCV-Based Intraday Signals in MNQ Futures: A Systematic Falsification Study
source.quote: The gross edge available to next-bar-open execution is constrained to approximately 0.07-1.50 points per trade ... A gap-continuation signal achieves T = 3.23 and +14.52 points but fails minimum sample requirements (N = 22) ... No signal satisfies all criteria simultaneously

prac-mnq-ohlcv-falsification.rule_formal: Falsification protocol: signal families (ORB, gap, volume, cross-session momentum, liquidity grab, vol-conditioned classifier) -> next-bar-open execution -> require min sample N + T-stat + net-of-cost expectancy simultaneously. Individual signal rules in full PDF (not extracted).

numbers.effect: gross edge 0.07-1.50 points/trade; best signal (gap continuation) +14.52 pts/trade, T=3.23, but N=22 fails sample minimum; zero signals pass all criteria
numbers.sample: MNQ 5-minute, 2021-2025, 947 trading days
numbers.caveats: index futures, not gold — XAUUSD session structure (Asian range) differs; 5-min not M1; abstract-level extraction (full PDF is image-encoded, not text-extracted); falsification framing means absence of edge in THEIR rule variants, not all variants

prac-mnq-ohlcv-falsification.mechanism: Claim is structural: information content of OHLCV bars alone (without order-flow/DOM) is too small relative to spread+slippage at intraday horizons on a liquid index future.
prac-mnq-ohlcv-falsification.relevance: Q1 direct and adversarial (liquidity grabs and breakout signals from bars alone were tested and failed on MNQ — sets a hostile prior for any Q1/Q4 candidate: demand it clear cost-adjusted, minimum-sample bars), Q4 (their 'liquidity grab' family is the formalized sweep)

test_design.lookahead_risk: their protocol (next-bar-open execution) is exactly the anti-lookahead discipline our sim uses — adoptable as-is

prac-mnq-ohlcv-falsification.overlap_risk: their volatility-conditioned classifier family overlaps rv percentile (falsified — consistent with their null result); gap family overlaps overnight gap ratio (falsified — again consistent)

verification.biggest_finding: arXiv 2605.04004 มีจริง (cross-check ทั้ง arxiv.org และ export.arxiv.org ให้ title/author/id ตรงกัน) — 'Structural Limits of OHLCV-Based Intraday Signals in MNQ Futures: A Systematic Falsification Study', Mathias Mesfin, submitted 5 May 2026, q-fin.TR · เลข 0.07-1.50 pts / T=3.23 / +14.52 pts / N=22 / 'No signal satisfies all criteria simultaneously' อยู่ใน abstract ครบ
