prac-mp-daytype-frequencies.title: Market Profile day-type frequencies: trending days only ~9.5% of all days
prac-mp-daytype-frequencies.claim: Under Market Profile classification, true trending structure (Trend Day + Double-Distribution Trend Day) occurs on about 9.54% of days; the rest are non-trending (Normal 2.43%, Normal Variation 41.77%, Neutral 30.21%, Non-trend 6.81%). A day-type filter must therefore be built to tolerate ~90% non-trend days.

source.label: TrendDayTrading.com — Market Profile Trading: Theory, Analysis and Practice
source.quote: Normal days ... 2.43% of the time ... [Normal Variation] 41.77% of the time ... [Neutral Day] 30.21% of the time ... [Non-trend Day] 6.81% ... Trend days are marked by a long skinny profile.

prac-mp-daytype-frequencies.rule_formal: Trend day (structural): one-timeframing (consecutive 30-min periods making higher highs+higher lows, or inverse) sustained open-to-close; long thin profile. Frequencies: P(trend-type day) = 9.54%; P(neutral) = 30.21%; P(normal variation) = 41.77%. No realtime numeric trigger given on this page — classification is end-of-day structural.

numbers.effect: Trending days 9.54% of all days; Normal 2.43%, Normal Variation 41.77%, Neutral 30.21%, Non-trend 6.81%
numbers.sample: population/instrument/period of the frequency study not stated on page
numbers.market: unstated (Market Profile literature is CME futures-derived)
numbers.caveats: classic MP frequencies circulate without primary attribution; page gives no instrument or years; classification is post-hoc (EOD) — realtime proxy (one-timeframing count, IB extension) must be constructed separately

prac-mp-daytype-frequencies.mechanism: Trend day = 'other-timeframe' participants in control from the open, continuously pulling in new participants; non-trend/neutral days = both sides near agreement on value, responsive trade dominates.
prac-mp-daytype-frequencies.relevance: Q2 direct (day-type taxonomy + base rates; realtime cue = one-timeframing on 30-min periods, countable before noon), Q3 (base rate: EXPAND days are structurally rare ~10%, so a filter that kills them kills the whole edge)

test_design.lookahead_risk: day-type label itself is EOD (lookahead); one-timeframing count at hour N is computable realtime from M1 — test must use the realtime proxy, not the label

prac-mp-daytype-frequencies.overlap_risk: one-timeframing/IB-extension proxy likely correlates with range expansion (falsified); base-rate framing itself has no overlap

verification.biggest_finding: เลขทุกตัว (2.43 / 41.77 / 30.21 / 6.81 / trend รวม 9.54) อยู่บนหน้าจริง และ trend 9.54% → non-trend ~90.5% สอดคล้อง '~90%' ของ claim แต่หน้าไม่ระบุ study/instrument/ช่วงปีใดๆ เลย และค้น cross-ref แล้วไม่พบแหล่งอิสระ (Dalton/CBOT/marketcalls/vtrender) ที่ให้เลขชุดนี้ — เลขปรากฏที่หน้านี้ที่เดียว
verification.note: provenance อ่อนจริงตามที่ claim flag ไว้: เป็นเลขลอยจาก single uncited source แหล่ง Market Profile อื่นให้แค่คำอธิบายเชิงคุณภาพของ day types ไม่ให้ frequency · ใช้ได้เฉพาะเชิง order-of-magnitude ว่า trend day เป็นส่วนน้อย (~10%) ห้ามใช้ทศนิยมสองตำแหน่งเหล่านี้เป็น parameter หรือ prior เชิงตัวเลข

prac-mql5-asian-box-ea.title: MQL5 Asian Breakout EA (article 17239): same strategy family as Trellis v4 — session box + MA filter + fixed RR; numbers only in screenshots
prac-mql5-asian-box-ea.claim: A published MQL5 EA implements the same architecture as our system — Asian session box (23:00-03:00 GMT high/low), stop orders beyond the box, 50-period MA trend filter, SL outside the box, TP at 1.3x risk, time exit 13:00 — and reports a 2023 backtest whose equity improved after adding a trailing stop; no numeric results appear in the article text (screenshots only).

source.label: MQL5.com — Automating Trading Strategies in MQL5 (Part 9): Building an Expert Advisor for the Asian Breakout Strategy
source.quote: place a Buy Stop order slightly above the top of the box ... a sell-stop order just below the bottom of the box ... [50-period MA trend filter; session 23:00-03:00 GMT; BreakoutOffsetPips = 10; TP multiplier 1.3; trade exit 13:00]

prac-mql5-asian-box-ea.rule_formal: Box = [min(Low), max(High)] over 23:00-03:00 GMT. BuyStop = BoxHigh + 10 pips if Price > SMA(50); SellStop = BoxLow - 10 pips if Price < SMA(50). SL just outside opposite box boundary; TP = Entry + (Entry - SL) * 1.3. Force-exit 13:00 GMT.
prac-mql5-asian-box-ea.mechanism: Asian-session consolidation defines resting liquidity beyond both extremes; London/NY participation resolves the box directionally.
prac-mql5-asian-box-ea.relevance: Comparative design point for the whole system (their box window 23:00-03:00 GMT is narrower than our 01-08 EET; RR fixed 1.3 vs our 1xR trail; exit 13:00 vs our EOD 23:00) — differences are testable knobs; trailing-stop-improves claim touches Q3

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — box complete before entry window

prac-mql5-asian-box-ea.overlap_risk: not a new variable — an alternative parameterization of the existing system (window/RR/exit-time); no overlap with falsified day-selection variables

prac-raschke-trendday-firsthalfhour.title: Raschke: trend day announces itself — first half-hour < 10% of day's total range, open/close at opposite extremes
prac-raschke-trendday-firsthalfhour.claim: On trend days the first half-hour of trading comprises less than 10% of the day's eventual total range, with minimal intraday retracement and momentum accelerating into the close; trend days are preceded by range contraction (NR7, 2-3 small-range cluster), hook days, or opening gaps, and the correct management is breakout-style entry held to market-on-close.

source.label: TradersLog — Capturing Trend Days (Linda Bradford Raschke)
source.quote: A trend day occurs when there is an expansion in the daily trading range and the open and close are near opposite extremes. ... The first half-hour of trading often comprises less than 10% of the day's total range. ... One of the easiest and more popular ways to exit a breakout trade is simply to exit 'Market-On-Close.'

prac-raschke-trendday-firsthalfhour.rule_formal: TrendDay (EOD): dayRange >> recent avg AND |Open-Close| ~ dayRange. Realtime cue: firstHalfHourRange / E[dayRange] < 0.10 (small early range = coiling, not chop). Antecedents (day-1): NR7 = narrowest range of last 7 days; cluster of 2-3 small daily ranges; hook day (open beyond prior day's high/low then reverse); opening gap. Management: breakout entry, no fading, exit MOC.

numbers.effect: first half-hour < 10% of day's total range on trend days
numbers.sample: descriptive practitioner article; no n, no win rate, no backtest
numbers.caveats: the <10% figure is a characterization of trend days, not a tested predictor; no false-positive rate given; 'expected day range' denominator must be estimated realtime (e.g. ATR), introducing its own noise

prac-raschke-trendday-firsthalfhour.mechanism: Range contraction leaves both sides under-positioned; an early directional imbalance (gap or one-sided open) forces the losing side and latecomers to chase all day — momentum builds into the close as trapped traders capitulate.
prac-raschke-trendday-firsthalfhour.relevance: Q2 direct (numeric before-noon cue: small first-hour range relative to expected day range + one-directional tape), Q3 (management of EXPAND days: hold to close, do not fade, do not tighten), Q1 (opening gap/hook-day context distinguishes real breakout energy)

test_design.lookahead_risk: first-half-hour range known by 08:30 EET at breakout window start; denominator (day's total range) is lookahead — must replace with ATR-based expectation at entry

prac-raschke-trendday-firsthalfhour.overlap_risk: antecedent leg (NR/small-range cluster) overlaps asian width ratio + rv percentile (falsified); the intraday first-hour-vs-expected-range cue is NOT among the five falsified variables — nearest neighbor is range expansion but measured at a different time anchor

verification.biggest_finding: ทุก QUOTE ตรง verbatim และหน้า traderslog ระบุผู้เขียนเป็น Linda Bradford Raschke จริง · attribution ยืนยันข้ามแหล่ง: บทความเดียวกัน mirror บน Forex Factory, investpost, blog อื่นๆ ในชื่อ 'Capturing Trend Days, by Linda Bradford Raschke' (LBR Group) — ไม่พบข้อขัดแย้งเรื่องผู้เขียน
verification.note: จุดอ่อนที่ต้อง flag: เลข '<10% ของ range ทั้งวัน' เป็น practitioner observation เชิงคุณภาพ (คำว่า 'often') ไม่มี dataset/sample size/ช่วงปีแนบใดๆ ในทุก mirror ที่เจอ — เป็น heuristic จากประสบการณ์ ไม่ใช่สถิติที่วัดได้ · ถ้าจะใช้ใน Trellis ต้อง re-measure บน XAUUSD tick data เองก่อน ห้ามยกเลข 10% ไปใช้ตรงๆ

prac-sweep-close-back-inside-1to3bars.title: Sweep vs genuine breakout: measurable rule = close back inside the swept level within 1-3 bars
prac-sweep-close-back-inside-1to3bars.claim: A liquidity sweep is distinguished from a genuine breakout by whether price closes back inside the swept level within 1-3 bars (with a long wick beyond the level and small body); vendor claims 65-75% win rate and 25-35% failure rate for confirmed sweep-and-reverse setups, but discloses no methodology.

source.label: CrossTrade — Liquidity Sweeps (price action education)
source.quote: Within 1-3 bars, price closes back below (above) the swept level ... a very long wick in the swept direction and a small body — a classic rejection candle. ... A sweep of a swing high in a strong uptrend is more likely to be a real breakout than a sweep-and-reverse.

prac-sweep-close-back-inside-1to3bars.rule_formal: Sweep(level L, up-side): High(t) > L AND Close(t+k) < L for some k in {0,1,2} (close back inside within 1-3 bars); else Break. Confirmation quality: wick beyond L >> body. Context veto: strong pre-existing trend in sweep direction -> treat as breakout, not sweep.

numbers.effect: claimed win rate 65-75%, avg RR 2:1 to 4:1, failure rate 25-35% for confirmed sweep-and-reverse
numbers.sample: not disclosed — no n, no period, no instrument
numbers.caveats: numbers are unattributed vendor claims with zero methodology — capture value is the operational 1-3 bar close-back-inside definition, not the win rate; the trend-context veto is consistent with our EMA-slope filter

prac-sweep-close-back-inside-1to3bars.mechanism: Stops resting beyond equal highs / obvious levels get filled into the hands of larger sellers; once that liquidity is consumed and price re-enters the range, the swept breakout entrants are trapped and must exit back through the level.
prac-sweep-close-back-inside-1to3bars.relevance: Q4 direct (first measurable sweep-vs-break rule: penetration + close-back-inside window at Asian high/low), Q1 (the same rule inverted = breakout confirmation: entry only if no close back inside within k bars)

test_design.lookahead_risk: waiting 1-3 bars after penetration delays entry by k bars — measurable at entry but shifts entry price; must test cost of delayed fill vs false-break avoided

prac-sweep-close-back-inside-1to3bars.overlap_risk: none of the five falsified variables measure post-penetration behavior; nearest system component is the breakout entry itself — this modifies entry timing rather than day selection

verification.biggest_finding: ยืนยันว่าหน้าไม่มี methodology จริง — เลข 'Win rate 65–75% / R:R 2:1–4:1 / fail 25–35%' อยู่ใต้หัวข้อ 'Realistic stats' โดยไม่มี backtest, ไม่มี dataset, ไม่มี citation ใดๆ และหน้าจบด้วย promo 'Automate your liquidity sweep strategy with CrossTrade' + ปุ่ม Start free trial = vendor page เต็มรูปแบบ
verification.note: Quote ทั้ง 4 ท่อนพบตรงตามอ้าง (1-3 bars close back / long wick small body / classic rejection candle / sweep ใน strong uptrend มีแนวโน้มเป็น real breakout). Claim ของ harvester ตีกรอบถูกแล้วว่า 'no methodology' — ผ่าน. คุณค่าที่ใช้ได้จากหน้านี้ = นิยาม operational ของ sweep (close กลับใน 1-3 แท่ง + wick ยาว body เล็ก) ซึ่ง testable บน data ของเราเอง · ห้ามยกเลข win rate 65-75% ไปใช้เป็น prior ใดๆ เพราะเป็นเลขลอยจาก vendor ที่มี incentive ขาย automation

prac-turtlesoup-whselfinvest-60pct.title: Turtle Soup (false-break fade) platform implementation: ~60% winners across FX pairs, GBP/USD 60.5%
prac-turtlesoup-whselfinvest-60pct.claim: Fading an N-period extreme touch (buy at 5-period low, sell at 5-period high) with an EMA trend-direction filter and 3x ATR(20) stop produces around 60% winning trades on major FX pairs (GBP/USD 60.5%) in the vendor's backtests.

source.label: WH SelfInvest — free trading strategy no. 38: Turtle Soup
source.quote: A buy signal is given when the market price hits a 5-period low. A short sell signal is given when the market price hits a 5-period high. ... The stop is calculated by NanoTrader as 3x the 20-period ATR ... The percentage of winning trades is around 60% for all currency pairs. For the forex pair GBP/USD the percentage is, for example, 60,5%.

prac-turtlesoup-whselfinvest-60pct.rule_formal: Long: price touches min(low, 5 periods) AND fastEMA > slowEMA. Short: price touches max(high, 5 periods) AND fastEMA < slowEMA. Time filter: signals only 06:00-22:00. Stop: 3 * ATR(20). Target: long exits at 5-period high (short at 5-period low).

numbers.effect: win rate ~60% across currency pairs; GBP/USD 60.5%
numbers.sample: vendor backtests on EUR/USD, EUR/GBP, EUR/CHF, EUR/JPY, GBP/USD; n trades and period not stated in text
numbers.caveats: vendor/platform marketing page; net return and profit factor not text-disclosed; wide stop (3x ATR20) vs near target (5-period extreme) means 60% win says nothing about expectancy; original Raschke Turtle Soup uses 20-day extremes, this is a 5-period variant

prac-turtlesoup-whselfinvest-60pct.mechanism: Breakout traders' stops cluster just beyond the N-period extreme; a touch-and-fail forces the freshly entered breakout crowd to liquidate back through the level, fueling the fade.
prac-turtlesoup-whselfinvest-60pct.relevance: Q1 + Q4 direct (formalizes sweep-vs-break as: extreme touched but trend filter opposes continuation -> fade; gives a concrete counter-hypothesis to test against our breakout entries at Asian high/low)

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — N-period extreme and EMA state known at touch

prac-turtlesoup-whselfinvest-60pct.overlap_risk: EMA direction filter overlaps our slope filter (slope percentile falsified, but slope sign is in the live system); extreme-touch component is orthogonal to all five falsified variables

verification.biggest_finding: QUOTE ตรงทุกท่อน (5-period low/high, EMA filter, stop 3x ATR(20), win ~60% / GBPUSD 60.5%) แต่หน้าไม่เปิดเผยอะไรเลยที่ใช้ตัดสิน expectancy ได้: ไม่มีช่วง backtest, ไม่มีจำนวนเทรด, ไม่มี avg win/avg loss, ไม่มี profit factor — win 60% คู่กับ stop กว้าง 3xATR สามารถเป็น expectancy ติดลบได้ (ชนะบ่อย-แพ้หนัก) และหน้าไม่มีข้อมูลหักล้างความเป็นไปได้นี้
verification.note: conflict of interest ชัด: WH SelfInvest ขาย platform NanoTrader ที่รันกลยุทธ์นี้ — เลข 60% เป็น vendor marketing ที่ตรวจซ้ำไม่ได้ · claim เรา label 'ตาม vendor backtest' ไว้ถูกแล้ว แต่ควรถือ EFFECT นี้เป็น unverified จนกว่าจะ replicate เอง โดยเฉพาะฝั่ง payoff ratio ที่หน้าจงใจไม่โชว์

prac-zarattini-orb-10r-moc.title: Zarattini/Aziz 5-min ORB: tiny stop + 10R target + market-on-close = 1,484% (leveraged) vs 169% buy-hold QQQ, 2016-2023
prac-zarattini-orb-10r-moc.claim: A first-5-minute-bar direction breakout on QQQ with stop at the first bar's extreme, profit target at 10x risk, 1%-of-equity risk sizing, and market-on-close exit if neither hits, returned 1,484% (with leverage via TQQQ) vs 169% for QQQ buy-and-hold over Jan 2016 - Feb 2023 — the payoff comes from letting rare expansion days run to an asymmetric target or the close.

source.label: Concretum Group (authors' site) — papers page for 'Can Day Trading Really Be Profitable?' (Zarattini & Aziz, SSRN 4416622)
source.quote: Results show that the ORB strategy, even with leverage constraints, significantly outperformed passive investing, yielding a 1,484% return compared to 169% for QQQ

prac-zarattini-orb-10r-moc.rule_formal: If Close(bar1_5min) > Open(bar1): buy at open of bar2; if lower: sell. Skip if flat. Stop = extreme of bar1 (low for long / high for short). Target = Entry + 10 * |Entry - Stop|. Size: risk 1% of equity to stop. If neither stop nor target hit: exit market-on-close. (Rules cross-checked verbatim on cxoadvisory.com review of same paper.)

numbers.effect: 1,484% total return (leverage-constrained/TQQQ implementation) vs 169% QQQ buy-and-hold
numbers.sample: QQQ 5-minute data, Jan 1 2016 - Feb 17 2023 (period per cxoadvisory.com review); includes two bear markets
numbers.caveats: not gold, not session-anchored; 33% annualized-alpha figure appears only in search snippets/paywalled review — not independently quoted, excluded; with 10R target the win rate is necessarily low, majority of days lose 1R — psychologically/structurally opposite to capping; later papers by same authors condition on 'stocks in play' (relative volume), suggesting raw version is weaker

prac-zarattini-orb-10r-moc.mechanism: Overnight-informed imbalance reveals itself in the first bar's direction; on the minority of days that trend (expansion days), the move extends many multiples of the first bar's range while risk stays capped at the first bar's extreme.
prac-zarattini-orb-10r-moc.relevance: Q3 direct (published-number template for 'dangerous but necessary EXPAND days': cap each day at small fixed R, let expansion days run to 10R or MOC — profits concentrate in tail days; contrast with our capped trail that RED-FLAG kills 2026 edge), Q2 (first-bar direction as earliest trend-day vote)

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — signal complete at first-bar close, execution next bar open

prac-zarattini-orb-10r-moc.overlap_risk: target/exit asymmetry is a management change, orthogonal to the five falsified day-selection variables; first-bar-direction component overlaps our breakout trigger itself

verification.biggest_finding: เลข 1,484% vs 169% (2016-2023) ตรงกับ abstract บน concretumgroup และตรงกับ paper จริง Zarattini & Aziz 'Can Day Trading Really Be Profitable?' (SSRN 4416622) รวมถึงกลไก 5-min ORB + stop ที่ extreme bar แรก + target 10R + MOC · counter-evidence สำคัญ: paper สมมติ no bid-ask spread / no slippage / no execution uncertainty — return 1,484% มาจาก TQQQ leverage บน alpha ~33%/ปี net commissions เท่านั้น (ไม่ net spread)
verification.note: ตัวเลขจริงตามแหล่ง แต่เป็น pre-cost-realistic: กลยุทธ์ยิงเข้าออกทุกวันบน leveraged ETF โดยไม่คิด spread/slippage ซึ่งกับ Trellis doctrine (#5 backtest หลอกตา) ถือว่ายังไม่ผ่านเกณฑ์ cost realistic · ยังมี regime dependence: 2016-2023 เป็นช่วง QQQ bull + vol สูงผิดปกติ ไม่พบ independent replication ที่ยืนยันเลขนี้หลังใส่ costs เต็ม (reviewer เช่น CXO Advisory / substack ชี้จุด slippage เดียวกัน)

caminschi-pmfix-leakage.title: PM gold fix leaked exploitable information to fixing members before publication (Caminschi-Heaney; primary paywalled)
caminschi-pmfix-leakage.claim: During the 5-bank London PM gold fixing era (pre-2015), the fixing process leaked economically significant, tradable information: fixing members' advantage was 'significant and economic' and grew as electronic derivatives (GC futures, GLD) made it faster to exploit — i.e., early fix-window flow predicted the fix direction before publication.

source.label: University of Western Australia research-impact summary of Caminschi & Heaney (2014), 'Fixing a Leaky Fixing', Journal of Futures Markets 34, 1003-1039
source.quote: The trade advantage available to fixing members was significant and economic, and it increased with the transition of electronic derivatives trading.

caminschi-pmfix-leakage.mechanism: The five fixing banks saw the client order imbalance inside the fixing call before the fixed price was published; trading desks with that knowledge traded GC/GLD ahead of publication — the forced/adversely-selected side is everyone providing liquidity in GC/GLD during the fixing minutes without seeing the imbalance.
caminschi-pmfix-leakage.relevance: Q2: mechanism behind systematic early-fix-window price moves (complements micro-lbma-pmfix-downward-drift which has the directional numbers); implies pre-2015 fix-window moves in the first minutes were informative continuations, and that this channel closed with the March 2015 electronic auction (regime split for backtests).

test_design.lookahead_risk: A 'first-minutes-of-fix direction' feature (e.g. 17:00-17:04 server move predicting 17:04-17:15 continuation, pre-2015 only) is computable in real time — no lookahead

caminschi-pmfix-leakage.overlap_risk: Shares the fix-window clock anchor with micro-lbma-pmfix-downward-drift (test jointly, not as independent evidence); orthogonal to rv/slope/range_exp/gap/aw
caminschi-pmfix-leakage.harvest_note: Primary paper paywalled (Wiley 402; SSRN/SemanticScholar/ResearchGate blocked or empty). The widely-cited numbers (return advantages in first 4 minutes; fix-direction prediction accuracy up to ~80-90%) could NOT be verbatim-verified from any fetched page and are deliberately EXCLUDED per anti-hallucination rules — parked as numbers: null.

gold-price-discovery-regimes.title: Gold price discovery leadership flips between London and New York in crisis-anchored regimes; final switch to NY in April 2011 (Lucey et al., LBMA Alchemist)
gold-price-discovery-regimes.claim: Which market SETS the gold price is regime-dependent: leadership switched between London spot and New York futures at macro-crisis dates (1990 Eastern Bloc, 1992 ERM, 1998 LTCM, post-9/11, 2008 GFC), with a final documented switch to New York dominance in April 2011 — despite London hosting 86% of trading volume. The liquidity structure our breakout trades into is not stationary across eras.

source.label: LBMA Alchemist issue 68, 'London or New York: Where Does the Gold Price Come From?' (summary of Hauptfleisch, Putnins & Lucey, Journal of Futures Markets 2016)
source.quote: London as a trading centre for gold with 86% of business occurring there, approximately 90% of which are spot transactions ... switch from the London markets to the New York markets occurs at the start of the ruble crisis and the bailout of Long-Term Capital Management ... final switch to New York in April 2011

gold-price-discovery-regimes.rule_formal: Information-share / price-discovery metrics (Hasbrouck-family) computed on London spot vs COMEX futures, January 1986 - end July 2012 (>6,500 obs): leadership is regime-switching with break dates 1990, 1992, 1998, 2001, 2008, Apr-2011; NY futures lead on average despite <1/10 of London volume.

numbers.effect: 86% of gold business transacted in London (~90% of that spot), yet NY futures lead price discovery on average; last leadership switch dated April 2011
numbers.sample: January 1986 - end of July 2012, >6,500 daily observations
numbers.market: London OTC spot vs COMEX gold futures
numbers.caveats: Sample ends July 2012 — says nothing directly about 2014/2017-2019 losing years; leadership metric is daily-frequency, not intraday-causal; Alchemist article is the authors' own popular summary of the JFM paper

gold-price-discovery-regimes.mechanism: Leveraged futures traders impound information first (cheap, transparent venue); OTC spot dealers are then forced to re-quote off COMEX to avoid being arbitraged — the forced side is London dealers hedging adverse-selection against futures-led moves. Regime flips at crises show the forced/leading roles swap when funding or risk conditions change venue economics.
gold-price-discovery-regimes.relevance: Q4 (core): documents that gold's liquidity/price-formation structure shifts in discrete eras at datable break points — the same class of structural non-stationarity suspected behind losing-year clusters 2012-14 vs 2017-19. Suggests testing strategy PnL against price-discovery-era dummies (pre/post Apr-2011 in-sample; motivates locating analogous later breaks, e.g. 2015 fix reform, with our own data).

test_design.lookahead_risk: Era dummies with historically datable break points are known ex ante going forward, but IN-SAMPLE era-splitting of our backtest is descriptive/diagnostic, not a tradable per-entry signal — lookahead-safe only as regime research, not as an entry filter

gold-price-discovery-regimes.overlap_risk: Era/regime variable — orthogonal to per-day rv/slope/range_exp/gap/aw by construction, but any era split will mechanically correlate with multi-year vol regimes (rv era means); guard against re-discovering 'high-vol years' under a new name

verification.biggest_finding: ข้อมูลของบทความจบที่ July 2012 (วิเคราะห์ Jan 1986–Jul 2012 แบบ rolling 100-day) — 'NY นำ' ยืนยันได้แค่ถึงกลางปี 2012 และประวัติในบทความเองแสดงว่า leadership สลับไปมาหลายรอบ ไม่มีหลักประกันว่า regime หลัง เม.ย. 2011 ถาวร
verification.note: ทั้ง 3 ท่อน quote ตรง verbatim (86% of business / ~90% spot / ruble crisis+LTCM switch / final switch April 2011) และหน้ารองรับ claim เรื่อง regime-switching ตาม crisis dates · counter-evidence ที่ harvester ไม่บอก: (1) บทความระบุเองว่า final switch เม.ย. 2011 'does not seem to have been triggered by any geopolitical or economic event' — จุดสลับไม่ได้ผูก crisis เสมอไป (2) 86% เป็นตัวเลข survey ครั้งเดียว (Table 1, LBMA loco-London survey) ไม่ใช่ค่าถาวร (3) บทความสรุปว่า dominance สลับซ้ำๆ ไม่ใช่ NY นำถาวร — ใช้ 'NY futures lead' เป็นสมมติฐานปัจจุบันต้องพึ่งงานหลัง 2012 (เช่น Hauptfleisch/Lucey/Putniņš 2016 ที่พบ COMEX นำ) ไม่ใช่หน้านี้

comex-settlement-vwap-window.title: COMEX gold daily settlement = 60-second VWAP window 13:29-13:30 ET; benchmark-tied flow must execute there
comex-settlement-vwap-window.claim: The COMEX gold active-month settlement price is the VWAP of trades in a fixed 60-second window (13:29:00-13:30:00 ET; calendar spreads 13:15-13:30 ET). Any participant benchmarked to settlement (roll/spread traders, funds marking to settle, TAS counterparties) is forced to concentrate executions into that window, creating a predictable volume/participation spike at a fixed clock time (~20:29-20:30 server EET most of the year).

source.label: CME Group Client Systems Wiki — Gold, Daily Settlement Procedure
source.quote: 13:29:00 to 13:30:00 ET for the active month and 13:15:00 to 13:30:00 ET for calendar spreads ... the active month settles to the volume-weighted average price (VWAP), rounded to the nearest tradable tick.

comex-settlement-vwap-window.rule_formal: settle = VWAP(trades in [13:29:00, 13:30:00] ET) for active month; fallback tiers use last trade vs 13:30 bid/ask, then prior settle, then GCC discretion. Server mapping: ET+7h = EET most of year (both DST, different switch dates -> +6h for ~2-3 weeks/yr). Testable: M1 tick-count at 20:29-20:30 server vs neighboring minutes.

numbers.effect: Settlement window = 60 seconds (active month), 15 minutes (calendar spreads); no published effect-size study on gold intraday vol/volume around this window was found (searched — parked honestly)
numbers.sample: Current CME settlement procedure (rule document, not an empirical sample)
numbers.caveats: This is a mechanical rule fact, not a measured price effect; ET-EET offset is +7h except brief DST-mismatch weeks (+6h) — clock mapping must be handled per-date; window sits just AFTER the 20:00 server entry cutoff, inside the holding period

comex-settlement-vwap-window.mechanism: Traders whose PnL/obligations reference the daily settlement (calendar-spread rollers in the 13:15-13:30 window, TAS trades that settle at settlement price, margin/NAV marks) have no discretion over timing — they are forced to transact inside the fixed window, and the roll period each contract month multiplies this forced spread flow.
comex-settlement-vwap-window.relevance: Q3 (core): pins the exact settlement mechanics and clock time; for us it predicts a recurring liquidity/volatility event at ~20:29-20:30 server while positions are held to 23:00 EOD — testable whether adverse excursions of held positions cluster at this minute, and whether futures-roll weeks amplify it.

test_design.lookahead_risk: Window time is known ex ante (clock-based, needs correct per-date ET->server conversion); roll-week calendar is known ex ante — no lookahead

comex-settlement-vwap-window.overlap_risk: Time-of-day event feature — orthogonal to rv/slope/range_exp/gap/aw; tick-count spike at the window will inflate same-day rv trivially (exclude the minute when computing day-level vol features)

verification.biggest_finding: หน้า CME wiki ไม่มีวันที่/ประวัติกติกาเลย — ยืนยัน VWAP 60 วิ ได้แน่แค่ยุคปัจจุบันย้อนถึง ~2016 (rule filing COMEX 16-574 แสดง methodology เดียวกัน) แต่ช่วงต้น sample 2012–2014 เป็นยุค floor/pit settlement — ไม่มีหลักฐานว่า Globex-VWAP 60 วิ ใช้บังคับตอนนั้น
verification.note: Quote verbatim ทั้งสองท่อน (settlement period 13:29–13:30 ET active / 13:15–13:30 ET spreads + VWAP rounded to nearest tradable tick) และหน้ารองรับ claim เรื่อง benchmark window เวลาตายตัวราว 13:30 ET · แต่ตามที่ claim เจาะไว้เอง: กติการายละเอียด (60-second Globex VWAP) ยืนยันย้อนหลังได้ถึงแค่ ~2015/2016 — ก่อนหน้านั้น settlement อิง pit trading แม้เวลาปิดจะอยู่ราว 13:30 ET เหมือนกัน · ใช้เป็น 'เวลา flow ตายตัว' ได้ทั้ง sample แต่ห้ามอ้างกลไก VWAP 60 วิ กับปี 2012–2014 · มี fallback tiers (last trade vs bid/ask, prior settle) เมื่อไม่มี trade ใน window

tick-volume-proxy-conflict.title: Tick volume as order-flow proxy: practitioner evidence claims 90%+ correlation with real spot FX volume, but is contested and non-peer-reviewed
tick-volume-proxy-conflict.claim: The best-known evidence for tick volume validity (Marney 2011, practitioner research) claims 90%+ correlation between tick activity and actual traded spot-FX volume; a counter-source examining futures volume finds tick volume 'mostly uncorrelated'. Net: tick count is defensible only as a RELATIVE intra-day activity proxy, not as a magnitude measure — no peer-reviewed gold-specific validation was found.

source.label: Global Prime (Medium), 'Why Is Tick Volume Important To Monitor In The Forex Market?' (citing Caspar Marney, 2011)
source.quote: striking high levels of accuracy between tick activity and actual traded volume ... correlations of 90% plus

tick-volume-proxy-conflict.rule_formal: Use tick_count(minute) only as normalized relative measure: z-score vs same-minute-of-day baseline or fraction of day total; never cross-day absolute comparisons (broker-feed dependent).

numbers.effect: Claimed correlation 90%+ between tick activity and actual traded volume (spot FX, Marney 2011)
numbers.sample: Marney 2011 practitioner research (webinar/paper, non-peer-reviewed); pairs not specified on fetched page
numbers.caveats: COUNTER-EVIDENCE fetched from EarnForex guide (https://www.earnforex.com/guides/tick-volume-in-forex/), verbatim: 'Tick volume seems to be mostly uncorrelated with real volume in the futures market' and 'One tick (price update) may be caused by either a small volume trade or a very large trade'. Neither source is peer-reviewed; no gold-specific validation found; Dukascopy col-6 tick-count usable relative-only per project F6 caveat

tick-volume-proxy-conflict.mechanism: Not a forced-flow claim — a measurement-validity claim (Q5 explicitly asks this). Causal link: dealers refresh quotes more often when order flow hits them, so quote-update intensity co-moves with trading activity; but one update can represent any trade size, so intensity ≈ activity, not signed volume.
tick-volume-proxy-conflict.relevance: Q5 (core): licenses using our M1 tick-count as a relative activity/participation feature in sweep-vs-break tests (e.g. tick-count z-score during the breakout minute), while blocking any design that needs true volume magnitude or signed order flow.

test_design.lookahead_risk: Tick-count of the breakout minute and prior minutes is available at entry — no lookahead; same-minute-of-day normalization baseline must be computed from PAST days only

tick-volume-proxy-conflict.overlap_risk: Day-aggregated tick count will correlate strongly with rv (dead variable) — value, if any, must come from event-level relative spikes (breakout-minute vs baseline), not day sums

verification.biggest_finding: สองฝั่งของ conflict มีจริงทั้งคู่ verbatim — ฝั่ง Medium/Global Prime: 'striking high levels of accuracy between tick activity and actual traded volume ... correlations of 90% plus' (อ้าง Caspar Marney 2011, spot FX, ข้อมูล eSignal/EBS/Hotspot ตาม secondary sources) · ฝั่ง earnforex: 'Tick volume seems to be mostly uncorrelated with real volume in the futures market.' และสรุปว่า tick volume ยัง 'unreliable for serious analysis'
verification.note: หา primary source ของ Marney 2011 ไม่เจอ (เป็นบทความ/presentation ยุค FX Trader Magazine ไม่ใช่ peer-reviewed — ทุกแหล่งที่พบเป็น secondary จาก broker/บทความ) และหน้า Medium เองก็เป็นบทความ broker (Global Prime) ที่ไม่แสดง methodology ของ Marney. สองฝั่งไม่ได้วัดสนามเดียวกัน: 90%+ วัด tick vs ECN volume ใน spot FX ส่วน 'mostly uncorrelated' วัด tick (broker) vs futures volume — conflict จึงไม่ใช่การหักล้างกันตรงๆ แต่ยืนยันข้อสรุปของ claim: ใช้ tick volume ได้แค่ relative/directional proxy ภายใน feed เดียวกัน ห้ามตีความเป็น absolute volume · หมายเหตุเพิ่ม: มี study อิสระของ Varianse (2022, PDF) ทำเรื่องเดียวกัน น่าเก็บเป็นแหล่งตรวจข้ามภายหลัง
